executive_summary: El problema está validado con intensidad alta (70/100): freelancers tech en LATAM enfrentan facturación fragmentada entre AFIP, SAT y DIAN, con 7+ workarounds activos, 9 alternativas pagas (ninguna cubre los 3 países para freelancers), y dolor reciente agravado por CFDI 4.0 y cambios en monotributo.
score_reasoning:
Score: 70/100
- Complaint Volume: 9/20 (15 unique threads found across Reddit aggregations, Argentine forums, and LATAM fintech blogs — solidly mid-tier in 6-20 range)
- Complaint Recency: 16/20 (80%+ from last 24 months; CFDI 4.0 migration, monotributo updates, and new DIAN requirements driving fresh pain; several sources from 2025-2026)
- Pain Intensity Signals: 13/20 (13 pain markers: "críptico y engorroso", "¿Es legal lo que estoy haciendo?", fear of AFIP sanctions, 74% freelancers paid late stat, currency conversion losses, "no puedo emitir factura" — 2 quantified costs, 0 explicit WTP statements)
- Workaround Evidence: 16/20 (7 distinct workarounds: Excel templates, Deel/Payoneer/Wise intermediaries, US LLC formation, manual AFIP portal, SAT free CFDI tool, hiring accountants, generic global tools — 2+ involving multi-tool stacks)
- Paid Alternatives: 16/20 (9 paid products: Alegra, Siigo/Aspel, Facturama, Gigstack, Abillio, Deel, FreshBooks, Zoho Invoice, Bind ERP — Alegra with 3.7/5 on Trustpilot, Facturama on Capterra, multiple with 10+ reviews)
Total: 9 + 16 + 13 + 16 + 16 = 70

data.problem_statement: Technology freelancers in Latin America (Argentina, Mexico, Colombia) struggle with fragmented, complex invoicing and tax compliance across three different tax authorities (AFIP, SAT, DIAN), each requiring specific electronic invoice formats (Factura E, CFDI 4.0, factura electrónica DIAN), leading to wasted time, fear of sanctions, and reliance on manual workarounds or generic tools not adapted to local regulations.
data.target_user: Technology freelancers in Latin America who invoice clients (especially international clients) and must comply with local tax regulations in Argentina, Mexico, and/or Colombia
data.evidence_summary: 15 unique complaint threads/posts found across Reddit aggregations, Argentine forums (MiPayo), and LATAM fintech blogs (Contablix, Wanderlancers, Takenos, Factoro, EdifactMx, Calim, Flexio, Belo, APX). Pattern: freelancers face a triple burden — (1) complex and country-specific electronic invoicing requirements (CFDI 4.0, Factura E, DIAN factura electrónica), (2) currency conversion and payment collection friction for international clients, (3) fear of tax authority sanctions for non-compliance. Existing tools either cover one country only or are generic global tools lacking local tax integration.

evidence.quote: Getting paid is the most frustrating part of freelancing — invoices getting ignored, processing fees too high, clunky software. Platform holding funds for 14+ days even after work is accepted.
¿Tengo que pagar impuestos? ¿Monotributo? ¿Ingresos Brutos? ¿IVA? ¿Ganancias? ¿Es legal lo que estoy haciendo? ¿Me pueden sancionar? ¿Se pesifican mis ingresos a dólar oficial?
With CFDI 4.0, the SAT verifies data in real time and any error invalidates the invoice. The system incorporates new validations and mandatory fields that must match SAT-registered information.
